description: Per-CLI-backend invocation policy + optimization config for mChatAIShell dev sessions. Each CLI has unique capabilities (MCP, sub-agents, model selection, context management) — this file is the single source of truth for how we configure them.

concurrency.description: Shell bridge has a 2-session concurrency cap. 'look-before-leap' = show BackgroundCommandCenterView when pool is full; user decides to cancel stale, cancel all, or wait.

capabilityProbe.description: Capability grid probe timing. probeOnLaunch: fire probeAll() in init. periodicIntervalSeconds: background re-probe interval (4 hours). staleThresholdSeconds: fire-and-forget background refresh in send() if older. neverProbedThresholdSeconds: AWAIT in send() if grid has never been populated (blocks until probe completes ~2s). awaitOnFirstSend: whether to block on probe in send() when grid is empty.

assetGathering.description: Phase AQ.GATHER: copy matched asset pack JSON + starter code into workDir before CLI session. The CLI agent reads these files directly instead of parsing prompt-injected strings. agentInstructionsTemplate references a prompt template in mchatai-source/harness/prompts/.

gemini.description: Workaround for Gemini CLI ImportProcessor parsing failures on blockquote-led context lines in injected GEMINI.md. Tune here via git (no shell/app rebuild required).

codex.unsupportedModelErrorSubstring: not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account
codex.description: If Codex rejects a selected model for the current auth mode, retry once with this fallback. Tunable via git so model-compat incidents are content-only patches.

claude.notes: Claude Code stream-json frames are line-delimited JSON objects with a top-level `type` field. `--verbose` is required when using --output-format stream-json (Claude Code refuses without it). Auto-approval writes y\n on tool_use; complete fires on `result` event. Assistant text nests under message.content[].text (array of blocks).

codex.notes: Codex CLI uses subcommand `exec --json` for non-interactive JSONL output. Confirmed shape (probed 2026-04-21): thread.started → turn.started → item.completed (item.type=agent_message|file_change|...) → turn.completed. Auto-approval via --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox flag (Codex's equivalent of Claude's --dangerously-skip-permissions). All payload-bearing events use type=item.completed, distinguished by item.type. The runner's awaitingApproval/toolUse/toolResult all share that same outer type — fine because Codex auto-approves with the bypass flag, the runner never needs to write y\n for Codex.

gemini.notes: Gemini CLI supports --output-format stream-json natively. Confirmed shape (probed 2026-04-21): init → message (role=user|assistant, content=string) → tool_use (tool_name, parameters) → tool_result (tool_id, status) → result (status=success). --yolo is the auto-approve flag (equivalent to Claude --dangerously-skip-permissions). assistantText extracts top-level `content` (Gemini stores text as a plain string, not nested blocks); requireField=role + requireValue=assistant scopes the extractor to assistant-only so user echo doesn't contaminate output. **WizardCLIRunner appends `goal` as the trailing positional argv** — Gemini accepts this via its `query` positional, so we DO NOT include `-p` in invocationFlags (`-p` consumes the IMMEDIATELY-following arg as its value, which collides with our trailing-positional convention and emits 'Not enough arguments following: p' + exit 1 with zero events). Probed 2026-04-22 after gemini-runner exit=1 canary failure.

optimization.notes: Claude Code has the richest toolset: MCP servers, sub-agents via --agents, prompt caching (automatic for CLAUDE.md content), worktree isolation, /compact for context management. Use opus for quality-critical generation, sonnet for iterative fixes. --add-dir expands file access beyond cwd.

Codex's --search flag enables native web_search tool (great for API research). gpt-5.4 is current flagship. --sandbox workspace-write is the safe default. codex exec for non-interactive, codex review for code review. Can act as MCP server itself (codex mcp-server).

Gemini 2.5 Pro has 1M token context — largest of all three. Best for whole-codebase analysis or massive refactors. --yolo for full auto. GEMINI.md is the project context file (equivalent to CLAUDE.md). --include-directories expands scope. Extensions and hooks provide pre/post-action automation.

taskProfiles.description: Which backend + flags to use for different task types. The routing ladder picks the first GREEN backend; these profiles tell it which optimization flags to apply.

codeGeneration.reason: Claude Code opus with sub-agents produces the best first-pass code quality

iterativeFix.reason: Sonnet is fast for targeted fixes where the context is already established

codebaseAnalysis.reason: Gemini 2.5 Pro's 1M context can ingest entire codebases in one shot

apiResearch.reason: Codex --search enables native web search for API docs and examples

refactor.reason: Large refactors benefit from Gemini's massive context window
